question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Sorry you hit all three — I’ll help triage and (if you want) open the required tickets and coordinate Network / UC / Identity / Print teams. Below I summarize the most likely causes from the Zurich incident records, give short, prioritized remediation steps you or on site staff can run immediately, list the exact artifacts I need to continue, and propose the escalations I will open for you. What’s most likely (based on past Zurich incidents) - Teams external calls silent (they hear you; you hear nothing): this matches a known Media Bypass + firewall hardening interaction where outbound UDP/session timeouts or missing RTP port ranges at the site edge caused RTP to be blocked or torn down (signalling completed but media died)

Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Scanner showed green check but vendor never received email: commonly the MFD accepted the job locally while the SMTP relay rejected or dropped it (examples: “Relay denied” for a printer VLAN not allow listed, or “Message size exceeds fixed limit” for large PDFs). Investigation requires the printer job log and SMTP/relay logs to correlate
context: SOURCE 6: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients Ticket number: TI-1624175 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 25 Owner: Employee 30 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 10.12.25 08:41 Closure Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04 Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored. Activities 10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25 Good morning At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails. Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally. Printing continues to function without issue. Could you please check this? Kind regards 10.12.25 08:41 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30. 10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis Test scan to internal mailbox → successful. Test scan to external address → no delivery. No error message visible on the printer itself. 10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network? 10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted. Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location. 11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis Internal delivery occurs directly. External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay. SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”. 11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0” Section Scan-to-Mail: • Printers must not send directly to the internet • Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay • Relay allows only defined IP ranges • DNS resolution must occur internally • Authentication must not be bypassed 12.12.25 – Further analysis Check of printer IP address. Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN. This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay. Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked. 15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list. 16.12.25 – Adjustment SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint. IP range of printer VLAN released. 17.12.25 – Test phase Scan to internal mailbox → successful Scan to external Gmail address → successful Scan to partner company → successful 18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 25 The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 36: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail fails for larger documents Ticket number: TI-1624682 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 28 Owner: Employee 34 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 15.01.26 08:57 Closure Closure date: 23.01.26 15:36 Closure text: SMTP and mail size limit adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Small documents (1–2 pages) work normally. Larger PDFs (approx. 20–30 pages) are not delivered internally or externally. No clear error message appears on the printer. Could you please check this? Kind regards 15.01.26 08:57 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 34. 15.01.26 09:30 – Initial analysis Test scan with one page → delivery successful. Test scan with 25 pages → no delivery. Printer displays “Send successful”, but no email in inbox. 15.01.26 10:20 – Mail log review Internal mail server logs show no incoming message for large files. SMTP relay log shows entry “Message size exceeds fixed limit”. 16.01.26 09:05 – Inquiry to customer Were there any recent changes to the mail environment or security policies? 16.01.26 10:10 – Customer response Two weeks ago, the maximum email size was reduced to limit spam. We were not aware that this could impact the printers. 17.01.26 – Comparison with blueprint “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0” Section Scan-to-Mail defines: • Sending occurs via dedicated SMTP relay • Maximum file size must be aligned with mail infrastructure • Changes to mail server policies must be coordinated with scan systems Finding: New limit = 10 MB Scanned PDFs exceed this limit at higher resolution. 20.01.26 – Further analysis Scan settings on device checked. Default resolution 600 dpi. PDF compression disabled. 21.01.26 – Test phase Temporary reduction of scan resolution to 300 dpi. Test scan with 25 pages → successful. Parallel coordination with mail team. Maximum size moderately increased. 22.01.26 – Configuration adjustment Scan profile adjusted according to blueprint: • Default resolution reduced • PDF compression enabled • Mail limit adjusted 23.01.26 14:50 – Customer feedback Several larger documents tested. Internal and external delivery successful. 23.01.26 15:36 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 28 The root cause was a discrepancy between the scan configuration and the reduced maximum email size in the SMTP relay. The settings were adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Scan-to-Mail now works reliably, including for larger documents. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.
